title: sophora chrysophylla subsp. glabrata
intro: Sophora chrysophylla subsp. glabrata, commonly known as the Pacific false indigo or glabrous sophora, is a plant species native to the Pacific coast of North America. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin problems, due to its health benefits, such as soothing and calming effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for medicinal use, with the dried roots and leaves being the most commonly used parts.
